title: Apply: Separation of Powers
description: Apply your knowledge of the separation of powers to this set of multiple-choice questions.

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from Montesquieu’s *The Spirit of the Laws.***

Democratic and [[☃ definition 1]] states are not in their own nature free. Political liberty is to be found only in moderate governments; and even in these it is not always found. It is there only when there is no abuse of power. But constant experience shows us that every man invested with power is [[☃ definition 2]] to abuse it, and to carry his authority as far as it will go. Is it not strange, though true, to say that virtue itself has need of limits?

To prevent this abuse, it is necessary from the very nature of things that power should be a check to power.

**Which statement best summarizes Montesquieu’s argument in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: relating to government by a small, privileged social class

choices.content: Abuses of power happen when those in power are too passionate about their political parties.
Virtuous people do not need checks on their power because they will always act in the best interest of others.
Successful governments give different powers to different people or groups to limit abuses by any individual.
Democratic governments have too much power and are not the best form of government.

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from Montesquieu’s *The Spirit of the Laws.***

Democratic and [[☃ definition 1]] states are not in their own nature free. Political liberty is to be found only in moderate governments; and even in these it is not always found. It is there only when there is no abuse of power. But constant experience shows us that every man invested with power is [[☃ definition 2]] to abuse it, and to carry his authority as far as it will go. Is it not strange, though true, to say that virtue itself has need of limits?

To prevent this abuse, it is necessary from the very nature of things that power should be a check to power.

**Which feature of the U.S. government best reflects the ideas in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: relating to government by a small, privileged social class

choices.content: The equal representation of states in the Senate
The addition of the Bill of Rights to the Constitution
The system of checks and balances
The powerful aristocracy in charge of the federal government

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from “Thoughts on Government” (1776) by John Adams.**

But shall the whole power of legislation rest in one Assembly? Most of the [[☃ definition 1]] reasons apply equally to prove that the legislative power ought to be more complex—to which we may add, that if the legislative power is wholly in one Assembly, and the executive in another, or in a single person, these two powers will oppose and [weaken] each other, until the contest shall end in war, and the whole power, legislative and executive, be [[☃ definition 2]] by the strongest.

The judicial power, in such case, could not [[☃ definition 3]], or hold the balance between the two contending powers, because the legislative would undermine it. And this shows the necessity too, of giving the executive power a negative upon the legislative, otherwise this will be continually encroaching upon that.

**Which statement best summarizes Adams’s argument in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The three branches of government work best when each branch gets specific powers and these powers do not overlap.
The executive needs to be able to check the power of the legislative branch, as the judiciary alone would not be able to resolve conflicts between these two branches.
The judicial branch needs to be strengthened, as the legislative and executive branches hold the majority of the power of the federal government.
The legislative branch is held accountable to the will of the people, making the issues it faces more complex than the issues that the other branches face.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which scenario is an example of how the judicial branch can check the power of the legislative branch?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Supreme Court strikes down a federal law because the law violates freedom of speech.
The Court of Appeals refuses to hear several cases challenging federal laws that ban racial discrimination in public places.
The House of Representatives initiates impeachment proceedings against a sitting Supreme Court justice.
Congress passes a law that creates five new district courts to handle an increase in the number of cases in the federal court system.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which scenario is an example of how the executive branch can check the power of the legislative branch?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The president opts to replace her running mate ahead of the presidential election to secure more voters.
The president orders the Environmental Protection Agency to enforce a new law on carbon emissions.
The president gives his State of the Union address, which details his goals for the year, in front of Congress.
The president vetoes a law passed by Congress that would increase the size of the Supreme Court to fifteen justices.

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from Federalist No. 51 by James Madison.**

In republican government, the legislative authority necessarily [[☃ definition 1]]. The remedy for this inconveniency is to divide the legislature into different branches; and to [[☃ definition 2]] them, by different modes of election and different principles of action, as little connected with each other as the nature of their common functions and their common dependence on the society will [[☃ definition 3]]. It may even be necessary to guard against dangerous [[☃ definition 4]] by still further precautions. As the weight of the legislative authority requires that it should be thus divided, the weakness of the executive may require, on the other hand, that it should be fortified.

**Which feature of the federal government does Madison argue for in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: has the most power or control
acts of intruding on someone else's area

choices.content: A bicameral legislature
A single executive
An independent judiciary
A weak federal government

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which scenario is an example of how the legislative branch can check the power of the executive branch?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congress orders the president to issue pardons for several former members of the legislative branch.
Congress votes to override a presidential veto of a bill that would use federal funds to create a system of universal child care.
The U.S. House of Representatives removes the Secretary of Labor from their position after he fails to implement new legislation.
The U.S. House of Representatives approves articles of impeachment against a sitting federal judge nominated by the president.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which scenario is an example of how the executive branch can check the power of the judicial branch?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The president creates a new court system between the Supreme Court and the Court of Appeals.
The president removes a case from the federal court system and assigns it to a judge in his home state of Montana.
The president issues an executive order to remove all current Supreme Court justices and eliminate the Supreme Court.
The president nominates a Supreme Court justice that aligns with her constitutional views.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which scenario is an example of how the judicial branch can check the power of the executive branch?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Supreme Court refuses to authorize a new treaty between the United States and Mexico.
The Supreme Court starts an impeachment trial of the sitting president, following several instances of presidential misconduct.
The Supreme Court rules that an executive order that punishes any person who publicly criticizes the president is unconstitutional.
The Supreme Court orders several executive agencies to stop their daily operations to balance the federal budget.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which scenario is an example of how the legislative branch can check the power of the judicial branch?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The House of Representatives initiates charges of impeachment against a federal judge after she is accused of taking bribes.
The Senate orders the Environmental Protection Agency to defy the Supreme Court’s ruling that an environmental law is unconstitutional.
Congress passes a law requiring the Supreme Court to rewrite past decisions that Congress deems unpopular.
Congress vetoes the Supreme Court’s ruling overturning a law that limits the amount of money a presidential candidate can raise during an election year.

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from *Two Treatises of Government* (1690) by John Locke.**

It may be too great a temptation to human [[☃ definition 1]] . . . for the same Persons who have the power of making laws, to have also in their hands the power to [[☃ definition 2]] them, whereby they may [[☃ definition 3]] themselves from Obedience to the Laws they make, and suit the Law, both in its making and execution, to their own private advantage. 

**Which statement best summarizes the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Those who make the laws should also have the power to carry them out, as legislators know the most about what actions are lawful.
Those who enforce the laws must work closely with legislators to make sure that the law is applied equally to all people.
Those who review laws for constitutionality cannot be in charge of creating the laws themselves, as they will never overturn their own laws.
Those who make the laws should not have the power to enforce them because they may not follow the laws or they may create laws that benefit them.

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from Federalist No. 47 by James Madison.**

The [[☃ definition 1]] of all powers, legislative, executive, and judiciary, in the same hands, whether of one, a few, or many, and whether [[☃ definition 2]], self-appointed, or [[☃ definition 3]], may justly be pronounced the very definition of tyranny. Were the federal Constitution, therefore, really chargeable with the accumulation of power, or with a mixture of powers, having a dangerous tendency to such an accumulation, no further arguments would be necessary to inspire a universal [[☃ definition 4]] of the system. I persuade myself, however, that it will be made apparent to every one, that the charge cannot be supported, and that the [[☃ definition 5]] on which it relies has been totally [[☃ definition 6]] and misapplied. In order to form correct ideas on this important subject, it will be proper to investigate the sense in which the preservation of liberty requires that the three great departments of power should be separate and distinct.

**Which statement best summarizes Madison’s argument in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: passed on from one family member to another

choices.content: The Constitution gives the federal government too much power and will result in a system similar to a tyrannical monarchy.
The Constitution does not create an oppressive government, as federal powers are divided among the three branches of government.
The Constitution makes the federal government weaker by ensuring that state governments can check federal power.
The Constitution creates an effective system, as concentrating power in one branch makes government more efficient.
